categories.name: Chemistry
categories.description: Required chemistry sequence. CHEM 3A requires previous or concurrent enrollment in MATH 2 or math placement. CHEM 4A/L requires CHEM 3A.

categories.name: Biotechnology and Society
categories.description: Required biotechnology and society courses. BME 80H: The Human Genome (Not offered 2025-26). BME 18: Scientific Principles of Life. BIOL 20A: Cell and Molecular Biology. BME 80G: Bioethics in the 21st Century.

categories.name: Statistics
categories.description: Choose one statistics course. STAT 7/L (Statistical Methods for Biological Sciences) is strongly preferred. Requires math placement score or MATH 3 or AM 3.

categories.name: Programming
categories.description: Choose one programming course. CSE 20: Beginning Programming in Python. ECE 80B: Engineering Innovations for Medicine and Natural Sciences. CSE 20 is waived for students who have already passed BME 160.

categories.name: Upper Division Core
categories.description: Complete all upper division core courses. BME 105: Genetics in the Genomics Era (prereq: BIOL 20A). BME 110: Computational Biology Tools (prereq: BME 105). BME 160: Research Programming in the Life Sciences (6 units, prereq: BIOL 20A).

categories.name: Upper Division Electives
categories.description: Three of the following courses must be taken. We recommend that two or more of the three are BME courses. BME 128, BME 178, and FMST 133 have additional prerequisites not covered by the major requirements. Students may petition to have one upper-division biology course count as an elective.

categories.name: Disciplinary Communication (DC)
categories.description: Complete the DC requirement. BME 185: Technical Writing for Biomolecular Engineers. Prereqs: ELWR and BIOL 20A.

categories.name: Comprehensive Requirement
categories.description: Complete the comprehensive requirement. BME 175: Entrepreneurship in Biotechnology.
